TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua aza sitsuayan ti sa Ḍiququ a se Kapayuanan.
S.PHON: izua aza siʦuajan ti sa ɖiququ a sə kapajuanan.
S.FORM: izua aza sicuayan ti sa driququ a se Kapayuanan.
S.PHON: izua aza siʦuajan ti sa ɖiququ a sə kapajuanan.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time there was a man of Kapayuanan called Ḍiququ.

S.FORM: muri livuin na se Qalu.
S.PHON: muri ɭivuin na sə qaɭu.
S.FORM: muri livuin na se Qalu.
S.PHON: muri ɭivuin na sə qaɭu.
S.TRANSL: East Paiwans were going to invade the area.

S.FORM: kidaringungu, sa daringunguin a djelapaɫ izua i Dinukapan.
S.PHON: kidariŋuŋu, sa dariŋuŋuin a ɉəɭapaʎ izua i dinukapan.
S.FORM: kidaringungu, sa daringunguin a djelapalj izua i Dinukapan.
S.PHON: kidariŋuŋu, sa dariŋuŋuin a ɉəɭapaʎ izua i dinukapan.
S.TRANSL: He got some (gum?) tree wood, and put it on the rock slab at Dinukapan.

S.FORM: mangtjez a se Qalu.
S.PHON: maŋcəz a sə qaɭu.
S.FORM: mangtjez a se Qalu.
S.PHON: maŋʦəz a sə qaɭu.

S.FORM: nu kidjulat tazua dinaringungu, sekulasu sa patsay.
S.PHON: nu kiɉuɭat tazua dinariŋuŋu, səkuɭasu sa paʦaj.
S.FORM: nu kidjulat tazua dinaringungu, sekulasu sa pacay.
S.PHON: nu kiɉuɭat tazua dinariŋuŋu, səkuɭasu sa paʦaj.
S.TRANSL: When they stepped on the place with the slippery wood, they fell off and died.

S.FORM: matsidiɫ anga a tjeza ta matsay.
S.PHON: maʦidiʎ aŋa a cəza ta maʦaj.
S.FORM: macidilj anga a tjeza ta macay.
S.PHON: maʦidiʎ aŋa a ʦəza ta maʦaj.
S.TRANSL: Only one survived (‘excluded from the dead’).

S.FORM: "sau vaiku," aya ti sa Ḍiququ tazua se Qalu.
S.PHON: "sau vaiku," aja ti sa ɖiququ tazua sə qaɭu.
S.FORM: "sau vaiku," aya ti sa driququ tazua se Qalu.
S.PHON: "sau vaiku," aja ti sa ɖiququ tazua sə qaɭu.
S.TRANSL: “Be off with you,” said Ḍiququ to this East Paiwan.

S.FORM: "ɫakua nu mangtjez sun izua i qinaɫan, tisun a matsay."
S.PHON: "ʎakua nu maŋcəz sun izua i qinaʎan, tisun a maʦaj."
S.FORM: "ljakua nu mangtjez sun izua i qinaljan, tisun a macay."
S.PHON: "ʎakua nu maŋʦəz sun izua i qinaʎan, tisun a maʦaj."
S.TRANSL: “But when you get back to your village, you will die.”

S.FORM: izua ti sa Ḍiququ anga kidjulat a pizua i Dinukapan; azua taiɫ a kula i Padain.
S.PHON: izua ti sa ɖiququ aŋa kiɉuɭat a pizua i dinukapan; azua taiʎ a kuɭa i padain.
S.FORM: izua ti sa driququ anga kidjulat a pizua i Dinukapan; azua tailj a kula i Padain.
S.PHON: izua ti sa ɖiququ aŋa kiɉuɭat a pizua i dinukapan; azua taiʎ a kuɭa i padain.
S.TRANSL: Ḍiququ himself (was so big that) when he had one foot at Dinukapan, his other foot was at Padain (across the valley, and he had a very long penis (OA)).

S.FORM: izua za ti Muakai a se Padain tjemenutjenun.
S.PHON: izua za ti muakai a sə padain cəmənucənun.
S.FORM: izua za ti Muakai a se Padain tjemenutjenun.
S.PHON: izua za ti muakai a sə padain ʦəmənuʦənun.

S.FORM: tevuta aza kulits i tju djeɫep.
S.PHON: təvuta aza kuɭiʦ i cu ɉəʎəp.
S.FORM: tevuta aza kulic i tju djeljep.
S.PHON: təvuta aza kuɭiʦ i ʦu ɉəʎəp.
S.TRANSL: A penis appeared along the wall.

S.FORM: "aki anema tsu?" aya mikeduts.
S.PHON: "aki anəma ʦu" aja mikəduʦ.
S.FORM: "aki anema cu?" aya mikeduc.
S.PHON: "aki anəma ʦu" aja mikəduʦ.

S.FORM: veɫatjen ta vaida.
S.PHON: vəʎacən ta vaida.
S.FORM: veljatjen ta vaida.
S.PHON: vəʎaʦən ta vaida.
S.TRANSL: She hit it with her weaver’s sword,

S.FORM: mangeḍuq aza kulits ni Ḍiququ.
S.PHON: maŋəɖuq aza kuɭiʦ ni ɖiququ.
S.FORM: mangedruq aza kulic ni driququ.
S.PHON: maŋəɖuq aza kuɭiʦ ni ɖiququ.
S.TRANSL: and Ḍiququ’s penis was cut off.

S.FORM: viɫviɫen nimadju aza kulits; namadjui anga namaɫavangas anga tua kinalaḍuq.
S.PHON: viʎviʎən nimaɉu aza kuɭiʦ; namaɉui aŋa namaʎavaŋas aŋa tua kinaɭaɖuq.
S.FORM: viljviljen nimadju aza kulic; namadjui anga namaljavangas anga tua kinaladruq.
S.PHON: viʎviʎən nimaɉu aza kuɭiʦ; namaɉui aŋa namaʎavaŋas aŋa tua kinaɭaɖuq.
S.TRANSL: He dragged it along, and it got thorns and burrs on it because it was so long.

S.FORM: semudjui semuɫavangas ti Ḍiququ, tjanu ita i tjakaz.
S.PHON: səmuɉui səmuʎavaŋas ti ɖiququ, canu ita i cakaz.
S.FORM: semudjui semuljavangas ti driququ, tjanu ita i tjakaz.
S.PHON: səmuɉui səmuʎavaŋas ti ɖiququ, ʦanu ita i ʦakaz.
S.TRANSL: When he pulled all the thorns and burrs out, they filled a winnowing basket.
